_doc: Pre-merge tomb_id corrections for chunk-31 (PM III.2 § II.G BETWEEN THE MONASTERY OF APA JEREMIAS AND THE ENCLOSURE OF SEKHEMKHET, printed pp.653-670, phys pp.293-310). One divergence across the three extraction agents: agent C emitted a longer descriptor `SAQ-TombPsammetheksAndKhedebneit` for the joint multi-burial 'TOMB OF THE PSAMMETHEKS AND KHEDEBNEIT-YERBONI [II]' on phys p.309 / printed p.669, while agents A and B both used the shorter `SAQ-TombPsammetheks` form. Per source-wide descriptor-tomb_id convention (chunks 22 SAQ-Shema, 24 SAQ-Sphinx-style descriptors) and chunk-30 SAQ-SankhenptahPtahshepses precedent: descriptor tomb_ids for joint multi-burial tombs are anchored to the FIRST listed occupant (or first one-two occupant names if needed for disambiguation); the second co_occupant goes in the co_occupants field, NOT in the tomb_id. The shorter `SAQ-TombPsammetheks` form follows this convention (the bracketed Khedebneit-yerboni [II] is recorded in co_occupants and notes_from_pm). Agent C's longer form would also work but creates inconsistency with the 2/3 majority canonical form. Canonical: `SAQ-TombPsammetheks`.

c|SAQ-TombPsammetheksAndKhedebneit.rationale: Agent C emitted SAQ-TombPsammetheksAndKhedebneit for the joint multi-burial tomb. Agents A and B emitted SAQ-TombPsammetheks (2/3 canonical form). Per descriptor-tomb_id convention: descriptors for joint multi-burial tombs anchor on the first listed occupant; subsequent occupants live in co_occupants. Cross-chunk anchor: chunk-30 SAQ-SankhenptahPtahshepses was a special-case 2-name descriptor used ONLY to disambiguate against an existing SAQ-Sankhenptah row from chunk-26; there is no homonymous SAQ-TombPsammetheks elsewhere in the source, so the 2-name expansion is unnecessary. Canonical: SAQ-TombPsammetheks.
